content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` is a placeholder ("TODO") and `NSCameraUsageDescription` ("This app needs camera access.") is generic with no feature tie-in (Info.plist).
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose permission strings are empty, placeholders, or too vague to tell the user what feature needs the data. The "TODO" location string will fail review outright, and the camera string doesn't mention document scanning even though ScannerView.swift:14 requests camera for exactly that. Replace them with specifics, e.g. "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and capture your documents," and either write a real location purpose or remove the key if location isn't actually used.
